calendar.matters:
Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
Off Calendar Apr-06-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. FRANK A. CIALONE, ESQ. of Shartsis Friese LLP (Tel: 415-421-6500) appearing for the Petitioner Shartsis Friese, LLP;
Atty. STEVEN D. MARTINI, ESQ. of The Martini Law Group, P.C. (Tel: 415-488-5046) appearing for the Respondent Gary P. Downs.

The matter is argued and submitted by counsel for parties. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

RULING: Petitioner's application for a writ of attachment under CCP 1281.8 is DENIED. Petitioner concedes it had the burden to prove that an arbitration award "may be rendered ineffectual without provisional relief" because respondent has "financial difficulties." (See id.) The evidence petitioner proffered with its motion was largely that respondent has not paid legal bills he owes it. However, petitioner repeatedly acknowledges this may be due to respondent's "unwillingness" to pay rather than any inability. Respondent countered with a declaration that his personal net worth is more than 45 times the amount he is said to owe petitioner. Petitioner complains that respondent's knowledge of his net worth is based on a document that was not adduced, but this is immaterial as petitioner - not respondent - had the burden of proof and did not carry it. Petitioner improperly submitted new evidence with its reply: a lawyer's speculation about claims against respondent that is internally inconsistent in any event.

The Court signs a formal order in open court.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR #6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
Off Calendar Mar-28-2018 Continued To Apr-06-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

PETITIONER SHARTSIS FRIESE LLP's Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment - Judge Kahn has recused himself from this case. The motion will be heard by Judge Ulmer on April 6, 2018 at 9:30am in Dept. 302.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Robert Balian, CSR#5220, email: robertbalian.csr@gmail.com.
